name: runner-escalation
prompt:
Implement model escalation for re-queued runner attempts.

In agentrail/runner/escalation.py expose:
- model_for_tier(tier: int, env: Mapping[str, str] = os.environ) -> str | None
- DEFAULT_ESCALATION_MODEL: str and ESCALATION_MODEL_ENV: str = "AGENTRAIL_ESCALATION_MODEL" (this exact string is the name of the environment variable that overrides the escalation model)

Intent: a previously red/error attempt is re-claimed at a higher tier and must run at a stronger model than the one that just failed. A tier of 0 or below (including negative) means 'no override' and resolves to None so the config default is used; a tier of 1 or more resolves to the escalation model — the value of the ESCALATION_MODEL_ENV variable when it is set to a non-blank string, otherwise DEFAULT_ESCALATION_MODEL (a blank or whitespace-only override falls back to the default). The override never applies to tier 0, which stays None even when the variable is set.

In agentrail/cli/commands/runner.py, in _make_execute(creds), the per-issue execute callback must thread the escalation model into the host runner returned by select_sandbox_runner(env): pass a model override for an escalated attempt and none for a tier-0 attempt. Only pass model if the selected runner's signature accepts it.
